ucs.title: [LLM] MirrorFace (Earth Kasha) Visual Studio Code remote-tunnel C2 abuse
ucs.description: Hunts MirrorFace's signature 2024 tradecraft of abusing the legitimate `code.exe tunnel` feature to establish covert command-and-control over `*.tunnels.api.visualstudio.com` / `*.devtunnels.ms` after spear-phish initial access. JPCERT/CC and Trend Micro have publicly reported this specific OPSEC pattern uniquely for MirrorFace / Earth Kasha campaigns against Japanese diplomatic and policy targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="code.exe" (Processes.process="*tunnel*" OR Processes.process="*--tunnel-name*" OR Processes.process="*service install*" OR Processes.process="*service-install*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(parent_process_name, "(?i)^(explorer\.exe|code\.exe|cmd\.exe)$") OR match(parent_process_name, "(?i)^(winword\.exe|excel\.exe|powerpnt\.exe|outlook\.exe|wscript\.exe|cscript\.exe|mshta\.exe|rundll32\.exe|powershell\.exe|pwsh\.exe)$") | join host type=outer [| tstats summariesonly=true count as tunnel_egress values(All_Traffic.dest) as tunnel_dst values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as tunnel_ports from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest="*.tunnels.api.visualstudio.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*.devtunnels.ms" OR All_Traffic.dest="global.rel.tunnels.api.visualstudio.com") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.app | rename All_Traffic.src as host All_Traffic.app as tunnel_app] | where tunnel_egress>0 | `convert_timeformat` | table firstTime lastTime host user parent_process_name parent_cmd cmdline tunnel_dst tunnel_ports tunnel_egress
ucs.kql:
// MirrorFace VS Code tunnel C2 — process + outbound correlation
let Lookback = 7d;
let TunnelDomains = dynamic(["tunnels.api.visualstudio.com","devtunnels.ms","global.rel.tunnels.api.visualstudio.com"]);
let SuspectParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","7zg.exe","7zfm.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let TunnelProcs = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName =~ "code.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("tunnel", "--tunnel-name", "service install", "service-install")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (SuspectParents)
       or InitiatingProcessParentFileName in~ (SuspectParents)
    | project ProcTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName,
              InitiatingProcessParentFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let TunnelNet = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (TunnelDomains)
       or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "code.exe"
          and (RemoteUrl has "tunnels." or RemoteUrl has "devtunnels.")
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl,
              RemotePort, NetProc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              NetCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
TunnelProcs
| join kind=inner TunnelNet on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + 30m)
| project ProcTime, NetTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, ProcTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          TunnelCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort, NetProc
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// MirrorFace VS Code tunnel C2 — Sentinel via SecurityEvent 4688 + CommonSecurityLog egress
let Lookback = 7d;
let TunnelDomains = dynamic(["tunnels.api.visualstudio.com","devtunnels.ms"]);
let SuspectParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe"]);
let TunnelProcs = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\code.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("tunnel", "--tunnel-name", "service install", "service-install")
    | extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ParentLeaf in~ (SuspectParents)
    | project ProcTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
let TunnelNet = CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where DestinationHostName has_any (TunnelDomains)
       or RequestURL has_any (TunnelDomains)
    | project NetTime = TimeGenerated, Computer = SourceHostName,
              DestinationHostName, RequestURL, DestinationIP, DestinationPort,
              RequestClientApplication;
TunnelProcs
| join kind=inner TunnelNet on Computer
| where NetTime between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + 30m)
| project ProcTime, NetTime, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, CommandLine,
          DestinationHostName, RequestURL, DestinationIP, DestinationPort
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MirrorFace VS Code Remote Tunnel C2 Establishment
id: 7e1fa3b2-3c5d-4f88-9b21-a4e6d6cf8af1
status: experimental
description: Detects abuse of `code.exe tunnel` for covert C2, a tradecraft pattern publicly attributed to MirrorFace / Earth Kasha (G1054).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1054/
  - https://blogs.jpcert.or.jp/en/2024/07/mirrorface-attack-against-japanese-organisations.html
author: SOC detection engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.t1102
  - attack.g1054
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith: '\code.exe'
  selection_cli:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' tunnel'
      - '--tunnel-name'
      - 'service install'
      - 'service-install'
  suspect_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
  condition: selection_image and selection_cli and suspect_parent
falsepositives:
  - Developers legitimately publishing VS Code tunnels (rare from Office/script parents)
level: high
ucs.rationale: VS Code remote-tunnel C2 was first publicly tied to MirrorFace by JPCERT/CC and corroborated by Trend Micro / ESET in their 2024 Earth Kasha reporting. Generic detections for `code.exe` produce huge dev noise; MirrorFace's pattern is uniquely characterised by (a) tunnel CLI flags, (b) a parent that is Office, a script host or a LOLBin (delivered via their typical Japanese-language spear-phish ZIP→

ucs.title: [LLM] MirrorFace LODEINFO / NOOPDOOR / ANEL DLL side-loading from user-writable directory
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical MirrorFace (menuPass subgroup) execution chain: a legitimately-signed third-party binary (frequently a Japanese-vendor product such as K7Computing AV, JustSystems Ichitaro, or a renamed `vfhost.exe`) is dropped into a user-writable path and side-loads a malicious DLL from the same folder, decrypting LODEINFO / NOOPDOOR / ANEL in memory. T1574.001 + T1027.013 are the actor's defining install-stage combination.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process) as proc_cmd values(Processes.process_path) as proc_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*") (Processes.process_name="K7SysMon.exe" OR Processes.process_name="K7TSecurity.exe" OR Processes.process_name="JSESPR.exe" OR Processes.process_name="jsschhlp.exe" OR Processes.process_name="vfhost.exe" OR Processes.process_name="mscorsvw.exe" OR Processes.signature="K7 Computing*" OR Processes.signature="JustSystems*" OR Processes.signature="Trend Micro*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true count as dll_loads values(Filesystem.file_name) as side_dll values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action="create" Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" by host Filesystem.process_id | rename Filesystem.process_id as process_id Filesystem.* as *] | where dll_loads>=1 | eval baseline_window=relative_time(now(), "-30d@d") | search NOT [search index=baselines source=mirrorface_sideload_baseline | fields host process_name side_dll] | table firstTime lastTime host user parent process_name proc_path side_dll dll_path
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// MirrorFace side-loading — Sentinel via SecurityEvent 4688 + ImFileEvent (no Defender XDR ImageLoad equivalent on raw connector)
let Lookback = 7d;
let UserWritable = dynamic([@"\users\", @"\appdata\", @"\programdata\", @"\public\", @"\temp\", @"\perflogs\", @"\downloads\"]);
let HostBins = dynamic(["k7sysmon.exe","k7tsecurity.exe","k7avmscn.exe","jsesprt.exe","jsschhlp.exe","jslogd.exe","vfhost.exe","mscorsvw.exe","jusched.exe"]);
let HostExec = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ImageLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
             ImagePathL = tolower(NewProcessName)
    | where ImageLeaf in~ (HostBins)
    | where ImagePathL has_any (UserWritable)
    | extend ImageDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(NewProcessName, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
    | project ProcTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, ImageLeaf, ImageDir, CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
let AdjacentDll = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".dll"
    | project FileTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              ActingProcessName, TargetFileName, TargetFilePath;
HostExec
| join kind=inner AdjacentDll on $left.Computer == $right.DvcHostname
| where tolower(TargetFilePath) startswith tolower(ImageDir)
| where FileTime between (ProcTime - 1d .. ProcTime + 5m)
| project ProcTime, FileTime, Computer, Account,
          HostBin = ImageLeaf, HostBinPath = NewProcessName,
          SideLoadedDLL = TargetFileName, DLLPath = TargetFilePath,
          DroppedBy = ActingProcessName, ParentProcessName, CommandLine
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.rationale: MirrorFace's loader/decryption chain has been documented across ~5 years of campaigns by JPCERT/CC, Trend Micro (Earth Kasha), Macnica, ITOCHU CTU and ESET (Operation AkaiRyu) using a recurring core technique: drop a legitimately-signed (and therefore Defender-trusted) Japanese-vendor binary to a user-writable path, then place a malicious DLL adjacent to it that gets loaded via classic search-orde
